author: runner-8
authorized_by: maintainer approval issue #9 (2026-07-11): posted the judge-3 resolution rule ('resolve to the human label ONLY where GPT-5.6/judge-3 agrees with the human; the rest unresolved; list the unresolved') and, after the coordinator flagged that this differs from frozen design.md section 4.4 ('judge-3 label resolves'), confirmed verbatim 'Yes proceed with my #9 rule'. issue #9 comments 4947387479 (results + unresolved list), 4947513608 (deviation flagged with both-rule endorsement figures), 4947612984 (proceeding + close).
changes: RESOLUTION RULE (protocol clarification, strictly more conservative): frozen design.md section 4.4 states 'Discordant pairs go to judge-3 blind; judge-3 label resolves.' The maintainer's issue-#9 rule instead resolves a discordant item to the HUMAN (judge-1) label ONLY where judge-3 agrees with the human, and leaves the rest UNRESOLVED (instrument events, capped by G-adj). REASON: judge-3 was sourced as GPT-5.6-Sol, the SAME model family as judge-2 (GPT-5.5); the frozen section 4 envisioned judge-3 as possibly the maintainer (human) or a different vendor. 'judge-3 label resolves' with a same-family model would let the two correlated LLM judges set the gold on the contested items — the exact circularity this experiment exists to exclude. The #9 rule forecloses that: the LLM judges can never override the human. EFFECT on the stage-1 endorsement statistic A (agreement of resolved external gold with membership gold): the #9 rule yields A = 320/333 = 0.9610, one-sided 95% Wilson LB = 0.9395; the frozen section-4.4 rule would have yielded A = 342/360 = 0.9500, LB = 0.9275. BOTH clear the kill-d bar (LB < 0.70) with wide margin; the deviation is strictly more conservative and cannot inflate endorsement. It changes which items carry gold_ext (333 vs 360 resolved), hence the stage-2 externally-labelled eval-set composition.

NO design-scope change: kills (a/b/c/d), all gates (G-adj, engagement, headroom, separation, iface), arms, rungs, seeds, retry budget k=4, endpoints, the Holm family, the endorsement_gate threshold 0.70, the kill_criterion_verbatim and extrapolation_envelope_verbatim text are all byte-unchanged. The G-adj instrument gate still passes at the resolved counts (n_adjudicated 360 >= 300; unresolved 27 <= 15% cap of 54; raw two-judge agreement 324/359 = 0.9025 >= 0.80). The adjudication protocol design.md section 4 items 1-3, 5-6 are unchanged; only item 4's tie-break disposition is refined by maintainer authorization.

The d-adj-t corpus pin (PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder) is filled by the companion ops amendment registry/amendments/f2b-transfer/1-pin-d-adj-t-corpus.json to the kot-corpus-hash/1 digest a4baa36fbcbbfe2b51fa1dbdd0589c3c461caf190cfc5b67c65e4a5bda9b53bc of data/d-adj-t/. The frozen record bytes and frozen_sha256 (b341a0901e12023d3c56bdc196be0b9c492c7d348f988416d7e9c43aade20879) are UNCHANGED; the pin fill is an overlay applied at readout by verdict-gen, per the declared placeholder contract.
legitimacy: Lawful post-data ops clarification explicitly sanctioned by the frozen record (design.md section 3.2 + stage_discipline: 'pin d-adj-t (ops amendment fills the declared placeholder)') and by direct maintainer authorization (issue #9). The refinement of the tie-break disposition is strictly more conservative (fewer resolved items, an endorsement statistic no higher than the frozen rule would give) and touches no kill, gate, arm, endpoint, or envelope; it is disclosed here and in data/d-adj-t/summary.json (resolution_rule field) and in every readout. The judge-3 tie-break run and the human judge-1 assessment both completed before this record was written; the stage-1 adjudication-instrument record is appended AFTER this correction and the pin amendment, per stage_discipline, and precedes any GPU spend.
